title: Revolut | Backend Engineer | Remote | Nov 2021 [Reject]
interview_process_overview: HR contacted the candidate regarding a position available at Revolut and arranged the interview.
Revolut conducts 2 Technical Rounds and 1 HR Round.
All technical rounds are based purely on Java (Java 8+); Java skills must be polished.
Spring Framework is not allowed during the interview.
Production-ready code is expected, and unit testing (JUnit) of the same must be done. Any IDE can be used.
Interviewers are from different time zones and provide good feedback during the interview.

questions.description: Create a LoadBalancer class with the following requirements:
requirements: Has a method to register backend instances.
Each backend instance address should be unique — it should not be possible to register the same address twice.
Load balancer should accept up to 10 backend instances.
The LoadBalancer will be released as a library.

questions.description: Develop an algorithm such that when invoking the LoadBalancer's get() method multiple times, it should return one backend instance chosen randomly between the registered ones.

requirements_summary: Unit tests using JUnit are required for both coding tasks

methods.name: register(BackendInstance input) -> boolean
methods.logic: Checks if size < 10 and address is not already in the map. If unique and under limit, registers; else returns false or prints 'Overload'.

methods.name: get() -> BackendInstance
methods.logic: Converts keySet to array, picks a random index using Random.nextInt(), and returns the corresponding BackendInstance.

test_methods.name: testDuplicate()
test_methods.description: Tests that registering the same BackendInstance twice returns true the first time and false the second time.

test_methods.name: testMaxSize()
test_methods.description: Tests that registering 11 instances allows the first 10 and rejects the 11th.

known_flaws: Code was not up to the mark; had many flaws.
Bug in get() method: used random.nextInt(10) instead of random.nextInt(number of already registered instances).
System.out.println used instead of throwing custom exceptions.
Max size (10) was hardcoded instead of being placed in a constants class or properties file.
Concurrency was not handled (should clarify and potentially use ConcurrentHashMap).
TDD (Test-Driven Development) approach was not followed.
Unit tests were only written for the first coding problem due to time constraints.

interview_rounds.details: Not reached (candidate was rejected after Round 1).
Not reached (candidate was rejected after Round 1).

advice_and_tips: Keep SOLID principles in mind during coding.
The interviewer emphasizes your approach and understanding more than just the code.
Follow TDD (Test-Driven Development): write tests first, then develop.
Avoid hardcoding constants — use a constants class or properties file.
Use custom exceptions with proper messages instead of System.out.println.
Clarify concurrency requirements upfront; use ConcurrentHashMap if needed.
Revolut is known for emphasizing the Strategy Design Pattern (O in SOLID).
candidate_reflection: It was a great experience. The candidate plans to apply again.

community_comments.author: Vyshnav Ramesh
community_comments.comment: Observations on code quality: follow TDD, avoid hardcoded size, replace System.out.println with custom exceptions, handle concurrency, and note that Revolut is known for the Strategy Design Pattern.

community_comments.author: Anonymous
community_comments.comment: Found a Medium article implementing a Concurrent Load Balancer.

community_comments.author: maveriq
community_comments.comment: Critical bug in the get() method: random.nextInt(10) should be random.nextInt(number of already registered instances).

community_comments.author: Coder
community_comments.comment: Asked how the LoadBalancer was implemented and requested ideas/approach.
